text: Adopt structural-parent taxonomy convention; demote 'UI' catch-all
description: Convention decision: top-level/area parents should be structural (areas of the product) and contain sub-parents or aims belonging strictly to that area — not a 200-child grab-bag. Concretely: demote the 'UI' parent (22dd4138) into its already-existing children (graph view, list view, watchdog UI/enhancements, project loading) and stop using it as a dumping ground. This is the human-decision half of the Rearrange UI aims goal (18263fc2); the reparent suggester provides the mechanics.

status.comment: Blocker confirmed by running suggest_reparents on 'list view' (32 children, 25 leaves): suggestions were low-confidence (scores 0.25-0.59, tiny margins, e.g. a list-flicker bug matched to 'watchdog panel improvements'). Cause: candidate sub-parents are auto-derived (children that happen to have children) = a messy mix of real areas and leaf tasks, not a defined taxonomy. Needs the human to define the real structural parents (the 18263fc2 set: graph view / list view+vim-keybindings / project loading / watchdog UI), then re-run suggest_reparents with explicit candidateParentIds. Reparent mechanics (b0fb3242) are done; this taxonomy decision is the remaining blocker for actually reparenting.
